description: Two-component regulatory systems are the primary means by which bacteria sense and respond to environmental changes. The EnvZ-OmpR system senses osmotic stress and regulates outer membrane porin expression (OmpF vs OmpC). This system demonstrates sophisticated signal processing through histidine kinase autophosphorylation, phosphotransfer to response regulator, and differential DNA binding affinity modulating gene expression.

name: Two-Component Signal Transduction (EnvZ-OmpR)
notes: This flowchart represents the EnvZ-OmpR two-component regulatory system with 35 unique nodes. Contains 2 OR logic gates (osmolarity decision, OmpR-P level check) and 1 AND gate (OmpR-P DNA binding requires both phosphorylation AND promoter accessibility). The system demonstrates reciprocal regulation where high osmolarity activates OmpC (small pores) while repressing OmpF (large pores), and low osmolarity produces the opposite effect. All molecular mechanisms have been validated against structural and biochemical studies.
scientificAccuracy: This flowchart is based on verified biochemical and genetic data from peer-reviewed literature. The EnvZ-OmpR system is one of the most thoroughly characterized two-component systems. All molecular mechanisms including kinase activity, phosphotransfer, and DNA binding have been validated against primary research sources including crystallographic and biochemical studies.
